revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蝦夷穴]]
'''Ezo cave''' (蝦夷穴) refers to horizontal caves located in '''mid-slope cliffs''', distributed across '''southern Tōhoku''', '''Kantō''', and '''Chūbu regions''' of Japan. It is also written as '''エゾ穴'''. Despite the name, these caves were '''not''' created by the '''Emishi''' (蝦夷); rather, they are '''burial chambers''' built from the '''Kofun period''' to the '''Nara period''', including '''{{ill|Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Corridor-Style Stone Chambers|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}''' (horizontal stone chamber tombs) and '''{{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Yokoanabos|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}''' (horizontal cave tombs). == Explanation == The name "Ezo cave" originates from the idea that these caves were '''dwelling sites of the Emishi (Ezo)''', a historical term used for the '''Ainu''' people. However, during the '''ancient period''', the Emishi were called '''"Ebisu" (エビス)''' or '''"Emishi" (エミシ)''', while the term '''"Ezo" (エゾ)''' only became common '''after the Emishi disappeared''' from the regions where these caves are found. The term '''"Ezo cave"''' (エゾ穴) did not exist during the Emishi era, and the caves were '''never referred to as "Emishi Ana" (エミシ穴) or "Ebisu Ana" (エビス穴)''', indicating that this name was given '''long after their disappearance''', possibly '''during the late Heian period'''.<ref name="#1">『名取市史』 53-56頁。</ref> Although there is '''no definitive evidence''', it is speculated that the name '''Ezo cave''' was assigned during the '''Edo period'''.<ref name="#2">『多賀城市史』1（原始・古代・中世） 184頁。</ref> When '''knowledge of kofun and yokoana tombs was lost''', these caves were '''mistakenly''' thought to be ancient dwellings. In '''Meiji-period archaeology''', the '''residence theory''' was proposed by '''Tsuboi Shōgorō''' (坪井正五郎), but today, they are '''universally recognized as burial tombs'''.<ref>『改訂郷土史事典6・宮城県』「エゾ穴の遠い歴史と謎」 13-15頁。</ref> While only a few kofun and yokoana tombs '''officially bear the name''' Ezo cave, such as the '''{{ill|Susoezoana Kofun|en|Susoezoana Kofun|qid=Q11664581|lt=Susoezoana Kofun|1=Susoezoana Kofun}}''', '''many local communities referred to similar tombs as "Ezo cave"'''. During the '''20th century''', in '''Miyagi Prefecture''', many '''yokoana tomb clusters''' in '''{{ill|Natori, Miyagi|en|Natori, Miyagi|qid=Q642018|lt=Natori, Miyagi|1=Natori, Miyagi}}''' were called '''Ezo cave'''.<ref name="#1"/> Additionally, tombs in '''{{ill|Tagajō|en|Tagajō|qid=Q653355|lt=Tagajō, Miyagi|1=Tagajō}}''', '''{{ill|Sendai|en|Sendai|qid=Q46747|lt=Sendai|1=Sendai}}''', '''{{ill|Shiroishi, Miyagi|en|Shiroishi, Miyagi|qid=Q823489|lt=Shiroishi, Miyagi|1=Shiroishi, Miyagi}}''', and '''{{ill|Rifu|en|Rifu|qid=Q1202986|lt=Rifu, Miyagi|1=Rifu}}''' were also known as Ezo cave. == Archaeological sites with "Ezo cave" in their name == * '''{{ill|Susoezoana Kofun|en|Susoezoana Kofun|qid=Q11664581|lt=Susoezoana Kofun|1=Susoezoana Kofun}}''' – {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|qid=Q695892|lt=Nanao, Ishikawa|1=Nanao, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, Suso Town * {{ill|Ōyasuba Kofun group|ja|小川蝦夷穴|Ōyasuba Kofun group=Ōyasuba Kofun group|lt=Ogawa Ezoana|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyasuba Kofun group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大安場古墳群}} – {{ill|Kōriyama|en|Kōriyama|qid=Q264206|lt=Kōriyama|1=Kōriyama}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, Tamura Town, Ogawa * {{ill|Ezoana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezoana Kofun|qq=}} – {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|en|Sukagawa, Fukushima|qid=Q819664|lt=Sukagawa|1=Sukagawa, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture, Wada Aza Ezo cave * {{ill|Ezoana Yokoanabo Cluster|ja|蝦夷穴横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezoana Yokoanabo Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Nakajima, Fukushima|en|Nakajima, Fukushima|qid=Q370487|lt=Nakajima, Fukushima|1=Nakajima, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture, Matsuzaki Aza Zentana == Archaeological sites referred to as "Ezo cave" == * {{ill|Ōshiro Yokoana Kofun Cluster|ja|大代横穴古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshiro Yokoana Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tagajō, Miyagi Prefecture<ref name="#2"/> * {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoanabos|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagoyama Yokoanabos|qq=}} – Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture<ref>琴田隼一郎 『最新版仙台全図』、東洋造画館、1912nd year 。有限会社イービー・風の時編集部・企画編集 『100th year 前の仙台を歩く 仙台地図散歩』、せんだい120アニバーサリー委員会、2009th year 。</ref> * {{ill|Kumanodō Yokoanabo Cluster|ja|熊野堂横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanodō Yokoanabo Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Natori, Miyagi Prefecture<ref>[https://natori-shiryokan.jp/ 名取市歴史民俗資料館]「[https://natori-shiryokan.jp/historicspot/231/ 熊野堂横穴墓群（くまのどうよこあなぼぐん）]」。2023rd year 2月閲覧。</ref> * {{ill|Kōriyama Yokoanabo Cluster|ja|郡山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōriyama Yokoanabo Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Shiroishi, Miyagi Prefecture == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Tagajō City History Compilation Committee''', *Tagajō City History Vol. 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)*, Tagajō City, 1997. * '''Natori City History Compilation Committee''', *Natori City History*, Natori City, Miyagi Prefecture, 1977. * '''Hisashi Sasa (Editor)''', *Revised Regional History Encyclopedia Vol. 6: Miyagi Prefecture*, Shōhei-sha, 1982.
* {{ill|Cave painting|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave painting|qq=}}
* {{ill|Standing Pit Tomb|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Standing Pit Tomb|qq=}}
* {{ill|stone slab tomb|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone slab tomb|qq=}}
* {{ill|Irregular ironware|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irregular ironware|qq=}}
* {{ill|Tsukushi script|ja|筑紫文字|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi script|qq=}}
* {{ill|Coastal Zenpokoenfun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Coastal Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}
* {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}
